stories.title: Mirroring Gaza, Israel is destroying towns and villages in southern Lebanon
stories.description: In southern Lebanon, towns near the border with Israel have been largely destroyed by Israeli demolitions and strikes. Israel says it has been attacking Hezbollah infrastructure, but civilian infrastructure has also been significantly affected.

stories.title: Trump says he spoke with Putin about a possible ceasefire in Ukraine
stories.description: President Trump said he talked to Russian President Putin Wednesday about the wars in Ukraine and Iran.

stories.title: Trump says he is weighing reducing American troop presence in Germany after Iran feud
stories.description: President Trump suggested he could soon reduce the U.S. military presence in Germany as he continues to feud with Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the U.S-Israel war against Iran.

stories.title: The Iran war now has a price tag ($25 billion), but still no end date
stories.description: The Pentagon estimates the war has cost $25 billion over the past two months. In congressional testimony, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth did not say when the war might end.

stories.title: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.

stories.title: Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack
stories.description: In the two months since the deadly strike, the US defence department has said only that the incident is under investigation.

stories.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
stories.description: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire
stories.description: The US announced three days ago that the ceasefire was extended by three weeks after talks.

stories.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.description: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.title: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.description: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.title: In Tehran, money is short and a return to war looms over daily life
stories.description: The BBC's Lyse Doucet reports from Iran, where the buzz of busy shopping streets masks deep uncertainty over the country's future.

stories.title: US: Hegseth under fire in Congress over Iran war as diplomacy stalls
stories.description: Pete Hegseth faced sharp criticism in Congress over launching the Iran conflict without approval, as Democrats condemned the war’s justification and its estimated $25 billion cost. Meanwhile, Donald Trump rejected Iran’s proposal on the Strait of Hormuz, leaving negotiations at a...

stories.title: Crude oil prices hit four-year high as Iran war threatens to drag on
stories.description: Brent crude has jumped to its highest level since the start of the Ukraine war after US President Donald Trump signalled that the American blockade on the Strait of Hormuz could last for months. Also in this edition: the US Federal Reserve prepares for a new chief as its board tr...

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: Iran-US standoff drags on
stories.description: Donald Trump says a US naval blockade on Iran could last for months, as the standoff between Washington and Tehran continues. The crisis has disrupted oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, sending prices to multi-year highs. FRANCE 24's correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah, has ...

stories.title: Middle East: Rising tensions on Israel’s border as Iran negotiations falter
stories.description: Tensions between Hezbollah and Israel are escalating after a reported breach of the “yellow line,” with Israel threatening further strikes. Talks between Iran and the US remain stalled, complicating efforts to contain the situation. Meanwhile, Israel faces a growing scandal over ...

stories.title: Iran crisis, World Cup tensions overshadow FIFA Congress in Vancouver
stories.description: Football officials gather in Vancouver on Thursday for FIFA’s 76th Congress, weeks before the expanded World Cup kicks off in North America. War in Iran, logistics and Russia’s ban top the agenda. Iranian federation officials left Canada abruptly, casting a shadow over the meetin...

stories.title: Middle East war live: US naval blockade ‘doomed to fail’, Iran’s president says
stories.description: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian said on Thursday that a US naval blockade on Iranian ports was “doomed to fail” and would increase regional instability. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

stories.title: Civilians or Hezbollah: Who did Israel hit on Lebanon’s ‘Black Wednesday’?
stories.description: Israel says it targeted Hezbollah, but experts say the evidence proves otherwise.

stories.title: Pakistan opens up road trade routes into Iran amid Hormuz blockade
stories.description: With shipping disrupted by US-Iran tensions, Islamabad activates overland corridor to move stranded cargo into Iran.

stories.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 62 as Trump asks Iran to ‘give up’?
stories.description: Trump says the US blockade of Iranian ports is working, urging Tehran to 'give up' amid tensions.

stories.title: Could the US-Iran war become a protracted ‘frozen’ conflict?
stories.description: In the absence of a permanent deal, the war between the US and Iran is becoming one of attrition despite huge costs.

stories.title: Iranian officials to miss FIFA Congress over alleged mistreatment in Canada
stories.description: Iran's Mehdi Taj-led delegation turns back at Toronto airport due to 'unacceptable behaviour of immigration officials'.

stories.title: Oil prices soar on fears of long supply disruption, US siege of Iran ports
stories.description: US President Trump meets with oil firms over ways to minimise impact on fuel supplies amid US blockade of Iranian ports.

stories.title: Pro-government rally in Tehran demands end to US threats
stories.description: Hundreds of Iranians have rallied in Tehran to demand an end to US threats and the ongoing blockade of Iranian ports.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump urges Tehran to ‘give up’; Ghalibaf mocks US strategy
stories.description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf ridicules US's economic pressure campaign as oil prices surge and Hormuz standoff continues.

stories.title: Mamdani calls on King Charles to return Koh-i-Noor diamond
stories.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani called for King Charles to “return” the prized Koh-i-Noor diamond, which the British Empire took from the Indian subcontinent in the 1800s, on the third day of the monarch’s state visit on Wednesday. Before greeting Charles and Queen Camilla at ...

stories.title: US turns down Iranian proposal to end war
stories.description: • Trump warns Iran to ‘get smart soon’, accept N-curbs; signals months-long extension of naval blockade• Tehran warns of ‘unprecedented military action’ over shipping curbs• USS Gerald R. Ford to return after 10-month deployment in ME TEHRAN/WASHIN­GTON: Amid reports about Iran’s...

stories.title: Pentagon chief spars with lawmakers over Iran war
stories.description: WASHINGTON: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth repeatedly clashed with Democratic lawmakers over the Iran conflict on Wednesday, in his first testimony to Congress since President Donald Trump launched the war more than two months ago. Appearing before the House Armed Services Com...

stories.title: 2 Jewish men stabbed in 'terrorist' attack in London
stories.description: Two Jewish men were stabbed on Wednesday in north London, an attack police declared a “terrorist incident” and which follows a series of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites. King Charles III, Prime Minister Keir Starmer and London mayor Sadiq Khan led the condemnation of the “ap...

stories.title: 'You'd be speaking French': King Charles roasts Trump at state dinner
stories.description: King Charles III gave US President Donald Trump a taste of his own medicine at a White House state dinner on Tuesday when he joked that without the British, Americans would be speaking French. As the heads of state traded jokes during their dinner toasts, Charles referenced previ...

stories.title: King Charles, Trump toast ties despite Iran tensions
stories.description: Britain’s King Charles and Donald Trump hailed their countries’ longstanding ties at a White House state dinner Tuesday – despite the US president claiming the monarch agreed with him on Iran’s nuclear weapons. Over a lavish meal, Charles echoed the tone of his earlier speech to ...

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: Iran president says U.S. naval blockade 'doomed to fail'
stories.description: Oil surges to four-year high; U.S. Defence Secretary Peter Hegseth questioned in first congressional appearance since the war began

stories.title: Why the UAE is leaving OPEC
stories.description: The decision comes as the UAE, among the alliance’s top three producers, chafes at quotas capping output at 3.2–3.4 million barrels per day despite capacity nearing five million; the timing is notable, with the country bearing the brunt of Iranian attacks, including over 2,800 dr...

stories.title: Israel-Iran war updates: Jaishankar holds talks with Iran's Foreign Minister on West Asia situation
stories.description: U.S. Congress will grill Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth on the West Asia conflict on Wednesday

stories.title: Congress confronts Hegseth for the first time since Iran war started
stories.description: Democrats quickly pivoted to the ballooning costs of the war, the huge drawdown of critical U.S. munitions and the bombing of a school that killed children

stories.title: Lebanon President says Israel must fully implement truce as strikes continue
stories.description: Israel "must first fully implement the ceasefire in order to move on to negotiations... Israeli attacks cannot continue as they are," Mr. Aoun said in a statement shared by the Presidency.

stories.title: Trump warns Iran better 'get smart soon' and accept nuclear deal
stories.description: U.S. could extend its naval blockade of Iran for months more, oil executives were told in a meeting with Mr. Trump, an official said, after press reports that he had rejected Iran's latest proposed deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: US fails to meet set goals by imposing naval blockade on Iran — President Pezeshkian
stories.description: "Any possible attempts to impose a naval blockade and restrictions are contrary to international law," Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said

stories.title: Over 30 mln could fall into poverty due to conflict over Iran — UNDP chief
stories.description: According to UNDP, the poverty problem amid the conflict over Iran and the resulting energy crisis will particularly affect sub-Saharan Africa, as well as some Asian countries such as Bangladesh and Cambodia

stories.title: Russia has no data on Iran’s alleged aspirations for nuclear weapons — Medvedev
stories.description: The Russian Security Council deputy chairman noted that Israel, unlike Iran, is allowed to have these weapons for some reason

stories.title: Iranian president says Persian Gulf security requires regional states’ cooperation
stories.description: Masoud Pezeshkian held the United States and Israel responsible for "any instability in the Persian Gulf"
